danger_signals.visual_form: Text notification/phone call from terrorists; bomb location unknown within building cross-section
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring immediate player intervention; potential for building damage if ransom not paid or bomb not found in time

danger_signals.visual_form: Tenant dissatisfaction indicators (inferred from gameplay mechanics)
danger_signals.meaning: Building deterioration state; tenants will vacate if stress remains unaddressed

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual bottlenecks in elevator traffic on cross-section view
danger_signals.meaning: Critical infrastructure failure affecting tenant satisfaction and tower viability

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual destruction of floors/facilities if bomb response fails
danger_signals.meaning: Severe consequence state resulting from unresolved danger event

safety_signals.visual_form: Smooth elevator movement patterns visible in cross-section view; express elevators serving full building height
safety_signals.meaning: Building infrastructure health; tenant comfort and retention indicator

safety_signals.visual_form: Medical centers, parking lots, recycling facilities visible on floor plan
safety_signals.meaning: Tenant needs fulfilled; stress reduction through amenity provision

safety_signals.visual_form: Hotel facilities with housekeeping presence indicated on cross-section
safety_signals.meaning: High-quality service state; attracts VIP visitors and positive ratings

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual tower star level display (1-5 stars, culminating in 'Tower' designation)
safety_signals.meaning: Overall building health and prestige state; progression toward victory condition

safety_signals.visual_form: Notification that VIP arrival is imminent; visible prepared facilities
safety_signals.meaning: Opportunity for tower advancement through positive visitor experience

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bomb object/hidden location within building floors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Bomb location scattered randomly throughout building interior; triggered by player building underground
Notification event; presence in hotel facilities; movement through tower cross-section

faction_indicators.color_identity: Color differentiation used between office-type and hotel-type buildings (noted in development)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed across floors by type; visible in cross-section with facility icons
Activated as response agent when bomb threat occurs; must locate bomb before detonation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Limited building options available; basic facilities only; tower appears sparse with few floors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Slow income generation; limited tenant attraction; restricted progression mechanics

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Expanding facility palette visible; mixed-use building with residential, office, commercial, and hotel floors; elevator system becomes complex
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased tenant demand variability; VIP visits begin occurring; elevator management becomes critical gameplay focus

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Cathedral structure at top of fully-developed tower; maximum floor count (100+ above ground, 9 below); all facility types present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Highest prestige designation ('Tower' rating); stable income generation; no further progression possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Text alert overlaid on cross-section view; bomb location indicator added to UI; security response visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Time pressure introduced; player must either pay ransom or coordinate bomb search; failure results in building damage visualization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Santa Claus and reindeer animation flying across tower skyline
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cosmetic event; no gameplay impact; establishes temporal progression and calendar system

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Gold treasure discovery notification; below-ground floor development visible in cross-section
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Significant funds reward; incentivizes basement construction risk-taking

environmental_storytelling.technique: Cross-sectional building view as narrative window
environmental_storytelling.example: Player views entire tower interior simultaneously, observing tenant distribution, facility placement, and traffic flow; this reveals the tower's 'health' through visual density and organization patterns
Office and hotel buildings rendered in distinct colors within cross-section view; communicates functional zoning and building purpose to player at glance

environmental_storytelling.technique: Elevator traffic as metaphor for building vitality
environmental_storytelling.example: Smooth, efficient elevator movement indicates healthy tower; congestion visualizes stress and dysfunction; emphasizes infrastructure as backbone of tower society
Absence of visual occupancy indicators (inferred) signals building deterioration and tenant departure; presence indicates health

environmental_storytelling.technique: Random event notifications as contextual story beats
environmental_storytelling.example: Bomb threats, treasure discoveries, and VIP arrivals interrupt normal gameplay flow; function as 'dramatic moments' that punctuate management rhythm and create narrative tension

environmental_storytelling.technique: Star rating system as visual progression meter
environmental_storytelling.example: 1-5 star display provides constant feedback on tower prestige; visual feedback loop demonstrates player's cumulative management success

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Remains in residence; continues generating income; contributes to star rating progression
Vacates property; stops generating income; damages tower star rating
Increases stress level; visible waiting time in elevator queues; contributes to dissatisfaction
Uses hotel facilities; navigates building efficiently; provides positive rating and star advancement
Provides negative rating; withholds star advancement; leaves unfavorable impression
Demands ransom via notification; bomb begins detonation countdown; forces immediate player decision
Searches building floors for explosive device; race against detonation timer; succeeds only with player assistance/coordination
Long wait times visualized; tenant stress increases; bottleneck visible in cross-section view
Rapid floor servicing; express elevators efficiently deployed; tenant stress decreases; smooth traffic patterns visible

notes: SimTower uses a side-view cross-section paradigm as its core manifestation layer. All world-state communication occurs through this single visual window—there is no exploration or movement through the building. The game's danger and safety signaling is entirely systemic: danger emerges from unmet tenant needs and random events (bombs), while safety is communicated through infrastructure efficiency and facility availability. Faction communication is minimal; instead, the game emphasizes role-state (tenant, VIP, terrorist, worker) over faction identity. The star rating system serves as both diegetic (in-world prestige) and non-diegetic (player progress feedback) communication. Visual differentiation between office and hotel buildings was a deliberate design choice to aid player cognition at scale. The game's sound design is minimalist ('office buzz' and elevator bells), leaving visuals to carry the primary communicative load.
